ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code for the country */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  "area_sq_km" REAL /* Area of the country in square kilometers */,
  "capital" TEXT /* Capital city of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "provinces" (
  "province_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each province */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the province belongs to */,
  "province_name" TEXT /* Name of the province */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the province */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the province */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the province */,
  "area_sq_km" REAL /* Area of the province in square kilometers */,
  "governor" TEXT /* Governor of the province */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("province_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_provinces_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cases" (
  "case_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each case */,
  "province_id" INTEGER /* ID of the province where the case was reported */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the case was reported */,
  "confirmed" INTEGER /* Number of confirmed cases */,
  "deaths" INTEGER /* Number of deaths */,
  "recoveries" INTEGER /* Number of recoveries */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the case */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cases_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cases_province_id FOREIGN KEY ("province_id") REFERENCES provinces ("province_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., health official, researcher, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user account */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "case_id" INTEGER /* ID of the case being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "query_params" TEXT /* Query parameters used for filtering the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_case_id FOREIGN KEY ("case_id") REFERENCES cases ("case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "send_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "read_status" INTEGER /* Whether the notification has been read by the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "geographic_divisions" (
  "division_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each geographic division */,
  "province_id" INTEGER /* ID of the province the division belongs to */,
  "division_name" TEXT /* Name of the geographic division */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the geographic division */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the geographic division */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the geographic division */,
  "area_sq_km" REAL /* Area of the geographic division in square kilometers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("division_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_geographic_divisions_province_id FOREIGN KEY ("province_id") REFERENCES provinces ("province_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "division_cases" (
  "division_case_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each division case */,
  "division_id" INTEGER /* ID of the geographic division where the case was reported */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the case was reported */,
  "confirmed" INTEGER /* Number of confirmed cases */,
  "deaths" INTEGER /* Number of deaths */,
  "recoveries" INTEGER /* Number of recoveries */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("division_case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_division_cases_division_id FOREIGN KEY ("division_id") REFERENCES geographic_divisions ("division_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_division_cases_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  "data_format" TEXT /* Format of the data (e.g., CSV, JSON) */,
  "update_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which the data is updated (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "case_geo_locations" (
  "case_id" INTEGER /* ID of the case */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the case */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the case */,
  "location_type" TEXT /* Type of location (e.g., city, province) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("case_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "case_symptoms" (
  "case_id" INTEGER /* ID of the case */,
  "symptom" TEXT /* Symptom reported by the case */,
  "symptom_date" TEXT /* Date the symptom was reported */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("case_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "case_contacts" (
  "case_id" INTEGER /* ID of the case */,
  "contact_id" INTEGER /* ID of the contact */,
  "contact_type" TEXT /* Type of contact (e.g., family, friend) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("case_id", "contact_id")
)
